name: Always Payday
description_short: Always Payday offers payday loans, title loans, check cashing, and small cash advances in Columbus, OH with same-day funding options.
description_long:
Always Payday is a cash advance and lending storefront located at 2084 E Dublin Granville Rd in Columbus, Ohio. The business operates as a traditional check-cashing and small-dollar loan provider serving the local community.

The company specializes in four primary financial services: payday loans (short-term unsecured loans), title loans (collateral-based loans using vehicle titles), check cashing services, and general loans. These products are designed to provide immediate access to cash for consumers facing emergency expenses or short-term financial gaps.

Always Payday distinguishes itself through extended operating hours, including Friday hours until 7:00 PM and Saturday availability (10 AM–3 PM), making their services accessible outside typical banking hours. Their physical location in Columbus provides face-to-face service and immediate transaction processing, which may appeal to consumers who prefer in-person financial interactions.

As an emergency cash lender, Always Payday fills a market niche for same-day or next-day funding but operates in a high-cost lending segment. Consumers using these services should be aware that payday and title loans typically carry significantly higher APRs and fees than traditional banks or credit unions, and debt cycles can result from repeated borrowing. The company's business model depends on short repayment terms that may not suit all borrowers' financial situations.
pros: Extended operating hours including Friday until 7 PM and Saturday 10 AM–3 PM for working consumers
Multiple loan products available including payday loans, title loans, and check cashing in one location
Immediate in-person service at physical storefront location in Columbus
Same-day or next-day funding capability for emergency cash needs
Accessible without requiring traditional bank account or credit score checks
cons: Payday and title loans carry significantly higher APR rates and fees than banks or credit unions
No website pricing transparency provided; APRs, fees, and loan terms not disclosed online
Title loans create risk of vehicle repossession if borrower cannot repay
Short repayment cycles (typically 2 weeks) can create debt cycles and repeat borrowing patterns
Limited to single physical location; no online application or digital lending option available
best_for: Consumers needing immediate cash ($100–$1,000) for unexpected emergencies when banks are closed
Vehicle owners with poor credit who can use a car title as collateral for larger emergency loans
Individuals without traditional bank accounts needing check cashing services
Workers seeking payday advances before next paycheck with no online banking access
diagnosis: Always Payday is best suited for consumers facing genuine emergency cash shortfalls of $100–$1,000 who need same-day funds and prefer face-to-face service. However, potential borrowers must understand that payday and title loans carry APRs significantly exceeding 36% annually, and debt cycles are common—making these products a costly short-term solution that should only be used when no alternatives (employer advance, credit union PAL, family loan) are available.
